message:
fix(drilldown): unify cluster overview tile navigation with lens buttons (#9009)

The ClusterDrillDown overview had an inconsistency between two similar
flows for expanding cluster resources:

- Clicking the Nodes stat tile scrolled to an inner branch of the
  Resource Tree, hiding the tab's lens buttons from view and making the
  list appear BELOW the Issues section.
- Clicking the Workloads lens button (inside the tab) rendered the
  namespaces branch in place with the lens buttons still visible, so
  the list appeared immediately ABOVE where Issues would have been.

As reported in #8975, this made the two interactions feel different:
clicking Nodes looked like a scroll-jump past the issues, while
clicking Workloads looked like an in-place expand.

Fix:
- Replace scrollToNodesSection with a single navigateToResourceTree
  helper that takes a TreeLens and activates that lens in the tab.
- Scroll the Resource Tree tab container into view (block: 'start')
  rather than an inner branch (block: 'nearest'), so both entry paths
  land on the same visible frame: lens buttons at top, selected branch
  below.
- Make the Pods stat tile symmetric with the Nodes tile by turning it
  into a button that jumps to the Workloads lens.
- Auto-expand the matching branch ('nodes' or 'namespaces') when
  arriving via a stat tile so the user immediately sees content.

Fixes #8975

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
